review_title: Storage hosting is easy with ShareFile
author: Zuhairah A.
pros: Sharefile is a cloud-hosted file server at its most basic level that replaces the need to host one within a workplace or business. It also allows you to offer download links to consumers to upload or download specific files, provided you have the proper rights. Because it is hosted offshore, it has the advantage of not requiring the office to operate servers or an Infrastructure rack.
cons: You won't be able to view your file structure if your external internet connection goes down. If you're a remote worker trying to connect to a server hosted by your company, this can happen. Another problem might be a lack of bandwidth while uploading/downloading to Sharefile. It was also quite sluggish and complicated when we initially started using it, requiring multiple periods longer to transfer or explore than a self-hosted server. We also have at least 1-2 service interruptions every month, during which users are unable to conduct their job for a while.

review_title: Great file sharing tool with strong security and audit options
description: If you have ever used dropbox, gdrive or onedrive you will find using sharefile easy to use. Major difference is that this product is built with multiple users and clients in mind from day one, while other mentioned products are meant to be single user products which have option of sharing documents under particular conditions. If we put security and audit ability aside (which are important to my use case), second feature really worth mentioning is practicaly unlimited ability to create clients who can access your shared resources. In my opinion (and research), there is nothing quite like it and ShareFile is great fit for my use case (and priced competitvely too).
author: Marko R.
pros: I am a long time user and I choose ShareFile because of security options and level of control I have over my uploads. Particulary, I have log file of who accessed what. I am located in EU and my files containt personal information it was really important to be sure that my data is kept within EU. I also like options to customized my sharefile experience which helps my client orient themselves more easily. At one point we also used ability of sharefile to create custom web forms for uploading, and that was a huge time and resource saver too.
cons: In almost ten years of using ShareFile I never had a single issue. There were few points in time when I had to warn my users to update their software, but web access was always there.

review_title: Used to be a great product, it is getting worse with every update
description: Used to be very positive, now I have nothing much positive to say about it, other than their customer service. The failed syncs are inexcusable and make the product virtually unusable. We are now looking into alternatives and have not moved to new providers just out of inertia rather than anything else.
author: Mario A.
pros: I used to like the fairly seamless integration and unlimited storage when I signed up. It could sync across users and devices very well an sharing with clients was also easy and auditable in terms of access control and visibility. A lot of those pros. are no longer there or have been eroded. Customer support are perhaps the only saving grace, when you eventually get through to them. They have tended to take ownership of issues and followed up appropriately every time. The fact they actually phone you to explain or check exactly what the problem may be is unusual and hugely positive. In that, they seem to care about their customers. In the functionality of the product, much less so.
cons: It is getting worse with every update. Half the time it does not sync across devices and users and requires saving a document in a different location or with a different name before anyone else can see it. Sometimes it does not even sync on the same device: I go to windows explorer and the version of the file I am working on has disappeared. The fact that, by stealth, they moved from "unlimited storage" as standard to 1TB per user (only unlimited in the sense you can buy extra space) is offputting. That they make out it was always that way is just insulting. At the current offering, we would never have signed up. There are better and more integrated platforms with more attractive pricing options. The repeated change of name is embarassing. Choose one and stick to it! "Offline files" are not really offline. When you first switch on a computer, it needs to have a connection to the internet before you can access offine files, which entirely defeats the point. Plus they are not normal files you can access like any other without the app, as all other cloud storage, unless the app is running, they are not accessible. Indexing within Windows environment, whether online or offline files, is slow, really slow. A search within sharefile, unless you go via the website, takes forever and half the time does not return results that are clearly there. Imposing 2-step authentication has also been a pain. However much I disable it everywhere, I constantly have to reenter domain name, email and password, plus wait for a code. Had to do it 3 times on the same machine today,

description: It is reliable for sending files (secured) and receiving large or confidential files. It got more room for improvement.
author: Liberty T.
pros: It was not pricey and the customer service is excellent and can be linked with our Microsoft Outlook which makes very handy, also knowing it makes secure file sharing. Requesting a signature is easy to use.
cons: Users (clients) had hard time to use especially to non-techy users. And the document upload sometimes feel a bit slow, especially when handling large files.

review_title: Share file is better now than when it was with citrix
description: It is good experience , I would recommend using it specially the cloud storage which facilitates the communication
author: Tarek E.
pros: It is easy to use , the support is very responsive and helpful Value for money and it has large storages
cons: It has bugs that sometimes it is very slow and sometimes users are not able to login The storage could be faster

review_title: Technically mature and secure product, customer service ABYSMAL!
description: The product itself offers a mature, secure cloud solution for document management. Recent customer service experience [Feb 2025] has been abysmal! I tried changing the terms of my renewal and literally spent a day trying to track down someone, anyone, to assist. By the time someone responded, the product had renewed with no option to change. Suffice to say we are moving away from it. Get your act together, CISCO/Citrix, or whoever currently owns/manages customer service and support.
author: Doc P.
pros: ShareFile security [256-bit AES] is top-notch and was the selling point for me to switch my practice over to it. Integration with both Mac OS and Windows made it a practical solution.
cons: - it does not integrate with virtual machines - there is a lag while sending out email in Outlook; not really sure what the connection is between ShareFile and Outlook - Customer service leaves a TON to be desired

review_title: Great File Server replacement for SMB companies and more
description: Excellent Every company needs to have cloud storage, Sharefile allows for this in an easy to use package. I like the 3rd party separation from the big players and the extra features that you would not get from a Microsoft or Amazon.
author: Thomas L.
pros: Easy to use and Fair pricing!!! We have been using this product for over 10 years successfully!!! Keep up the good work...
cons: Desktop app will glitch if overloaded To fix the PC would need to be rebooted, as an alternate way to access your files, the browser will always work.

review_title: Simple for end users, powerful for admins
description: We have been using ShareFile for several years now to request data from customers migrating to our web applications. We especially like the simplicity for end users and the way data retention/link expiration works. I do think the product is a little expensive but I haven't found anything that works as well.
author: Mark C.
pros: The simplicity for end users makes ShareFile a great choice for our business case as we have none technical staff receiving requests for data uploads.
cons: The authentication process when logging in was a little flaky, we sometimes had to log in twice. This looks to have been updated recently.

description: The Progress Sharefile (Previous Citrux Sharefile), is unreliable in a way that it has system-wide slowdowns. Probably due to the limited resources allocated. Not for file share, but if you are using their projects. It works well in the off-season. However, right before the deadline, you keep on getting those errors and endlessly press Ctrl 5. It cripples the whole tax preparation process when you cannot retrieve data from the document requests or information requests. When you reach out to the support, they never understand the issue. In short, not a reliable product at all especially for a tax prep firm. Imagine right before the deadline struggling to get the data. The dreadful calls to clients to send the data through alternate means. Very disappointing.
author: MAROOF S.
pros: The new features they introduce. (provided they work)
cons: System availability during peak time. No document requests, information requests or projects open during peak of the tax season.

description: Very Nice we are using this platform as a mediator with our client. we are using 20 Administrator plan and very much satisfied. 20 TB storage is good enough for small organisation and there is an option available to increase the storage space it will more beneficial in case if we require. Overall its good.
author: Nilesh P.
pros: Very nice app in this era for cloud storage in average cost. Folder Management is good. We can create and share with user's ID. Its also allowing us to share with links and with proper validity. In simple words its good to go.
cons: Responding very slow Like login and surfing in folders. As our experience with other cloud storage Sharefile take much time to for login authentication. Support team should rectify the speed and it may good to use in market if slowness remove.

description: I've had a positive experience using Sharefile over the past year. I like how simple it is to send out documents for signature and the encryption & security options are quite useful. I've also used it to store files and create folders, so it is also a great website for database management.
author: George P.
pros: Versatility. Good platform for creating and sharing folders with other users. Great for database management for documents and easy to send out documents for signature. Decent security features as well for sensitive documents.
cons: It can be slow and unresponsive at times.

description: Positive, lightweight program that is secure and intuitive
author: michael G.
pros: Allows secure secure transfer of documents with clients
cons: Can be confusing for some clients to authenticaate

description: ShareFile is irreplaceable for us. It simplifies file sharing while ensuring security.
author: Vallery O.
pros: ShareFile is incredibly user-friendly and straightforward to implement. Its customizable security options ensure that sensitive information is well-protected, giving us peace of mind. I also love that I get alerts when files are viewed or downloaded, allowing for better tracking and management of shared documents. One of the standout features is that users don’t need to log in to view files, which simplifies the process for recipients and enhances overall accessibility.
cons: I really have no negatives to mention—it's just an incredibly useful tool! It has streamlined our processes and made collaboration so much easier. I can’t imagine working without it!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great as a File Storage System
description: We put Sharefile in place without an IT admin or any IT specialists on hand. A group of four of us tackled the transition from our university's shared drive, handling the data migration and the set up of folder structures. Because of this, I'm sure we didn't have all file storage best practices down pat. I don't know that Citrix provides much in way of recommendations for how to structure the data; that said, we only purchased Sharefile rather than a more enterprise-minded solution. I am sure with heftier purchases that there'd be more consultation and interaction. For the several-thousand annual price we pay for a team of around 40, the benefits of Sharefile are immense. As with any system of this size that's cloud-based, there's a bit of a learning curve to get comfortable with the more in-depth features and functionality, but once we learned how certain aspects work, it's been great and has become a core part of how we conduct the sharing of files with clients.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Cloud-based and easy to understand web interface is nice. Onboarding and implementation was good and inexpensive. The check-in and check-out file features are nice, as are the abilities to share files with external users. The security and uptime as well as ability to resolve any system-wide outages quickly.
cons: Some frustrations in features that I was expecting to be more fully fleshed out, such as workflows or ability to work on documents online without the lag of connecting with 365 and handling some of those handovers.

description: Overall, sharefile is a great tool if you need to quickly find a file sharing solution and a way to send files securely vs. sending items through e-mail attachments. The alerting/notification features are also nice so users are reminded when new information is available to download and review. It's great for file sharing, but there may be better options if you intend to perform an entire audit within sharefile as documentation can get voluminous. Sharefile is a great document portal to send files here and there as needed, but it really shouldn't be used as the interface for a working file if multiple users need to access and work within at the same time.
author: Jason P.
pros: The software offers alot of benefit in sending files securely between parties, and offering a shared drive between internal and external users to exchange documentation for purposes of performing professional services (audits, tax, consulting, etc.) It's intuitive to use and set permissions and access control as an administrator. Alerts and notification settings are also useful to alert users when information is available.
cons: Unlike other similar offerings, users can't work in the same files at the same time without causing the system to save new versions of the file. This can be frustrating when multiple users need to access and work on the same file as needed, and no ones work overrides others when trying to save the file. Additionally, it's sometime difficult to tell what folder contain files as Citrix doesnt show the size (MB) of any folders, the web browser version does, but the browser is less user friendly than navigating the shared drive structures through the citrix addon.

review_title: Turned a file sharing mess into an easy to use hub for our plants.
description: It cut down the time it took our workers to properly catalogue and upload video records. It also was easier to track down mis-labled records and put them where they needed to be. Having switched all our plants over to Sharefile, we are better able to view and respond to issues promptly. It's ease of use has left us with fewer errors in our documentation.
author: Levi F.
pros: The web interface was easy to pick up on. We migrated to Sharefile from another large file sharing site that was falling short in what we needed for our multiple plants. Sharefile gave a central space to the large amount of video files we use for inspections and documentation of various equipment that we own and lease. Having a central space to record and track all inspections was fundamental to our business needs. What impressed me most about Sharefile was the cleanliness of the website and the ease of use that it exemplifies. It took less than an hour to get all our workers on board with the how to use the site and catalog our files.
cons: The only real down side is with the gross amount of folders we have, you have to wait for the page to populate when you reach the end of the first set. Enabling a manual shortcut (like typing in a folder name to take you to that folder) would be a great benefit.

review_title: A Good software to share files
description: Overall it's a very good tool and does everything a company requires. We have been sharing files and collaborating with the help of this tool making professional lives easier. It has a good cloud storage and is completely secured.
author: Abrar K.
pros: It's very easy to use and doesn't require any prior knowledge. The file synchronisation is very good and it allows us to share files anywhere. We can generate links of the files and send them as email. We can set access controls to each file and restrict others from accessing it.
cons: We can view zip files in read only format and is a drawback. The uploading speed of the files is very slow and needs to be improved. It is expensive when compared with other competitors out there.

description: Sharefile made file sharing super easy and accessible for the office. Large files and folders can both be uploaded, which makes organization easy for all.
author: Martina C.
pros: The drag and drop feature makes ShareFile one of the easiest applications to use.
cons: Sharefile was easy to use. I have no complaints.

author: Patrick N.
pros: ease of use and deployment are highlights here. lots of features and ways to customize
cons: some things about the program are glitchy and/or hard to decipher

author: Virginia M.
pros: I love the simplicity of using this program and how quick I was able to implement it in my business
cons: The price has crept up slowly and I have been able to absorb it in my business but Im not sure how long it will last if it keeps increasing in price

author: Nurain O.
pros: ShareFile offer a quick and straightforward process that help in sending a files or document, with no size limit imposed n the files size. It is the best solution for anyone that need a simple way to moving their files around without having to register an account with the software.
cons: One of thing that i think that ShareFile need to upgrade is the file upload speed. It is too slow and will taking too much time for a large files thus making me frustrating more when the process get interrupted and had to repeating the same process again.

review_title: I deployed ShareFile on my platform
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: ShareFile is easy to use. The Outlook plug-in allows an easy way for customers to send secure files as well as secure emails with a few short clicks. Users are able to create their own folders and we share folders with our clients. There are templates for subfolders as well as retention periods for files. We are able to send attachments beyond Outlook's size limitations and ShareFile helps scan for potentially dangerous content. You can also control what network users access their ShareFile account. There are a lot of options available if you're looking to protect information that leaves your company.
cons: There can be issues with downloading a lot of files at once from the browser. However; Citrix makes a ShareFile desktop app this helps speed up this process. You can turn two factor on, but you have to have each user go to their account and manually enable it. That makes us work with each user in order to enable two factor. The cost can be expensive, but we had reduced rate. Its well worth looking around for a good deal if you decide to implement ShareFile. A small downside is when a user goes to preview a password protected document, they have to download the file because ShareFile's website cannot prompt you for the password.

description: We began looking for an enterprise solution to file sharing/signing and was hoping to reduce user burden by having the solution serve a host of functions. We were particularly intrigued by the prospect of secure file collaboration but this feature was not as user friendly and compared to Google docs, there was a lot lacking. While this prevented widespread implementation, several teams nonetheless still rely on sharefile. The continued acquisitions by Citrix also make one wonder how Sharefile will continue to improve/integrate over the next iterations.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Being able to send clients to a personal, branded client folder to access whatever they need (with my customized security settings and email alerts) was amazing. Also a huge fan of the send me a file link that can be embedded into email signature blocks
cons: The 2 main shortcomings of the product are what keeps it from being an Enterprise wide solution (though it is nonetheless superb for secure document sharing and signing). I did not test the Outlook plugin, but the Gmail plugin was not nearly as easy as it was made out to be.

author: Charles M.
pros: Sharefile allows ease of secure storage for all file types and an easy way to share them with others for download.
cons: How slow it is to search through the thousands and thousands of files we store on Sharefile - honestly, nothing

author: Tapan J.
pros: Sharefile has attempted to enable the users share files larger than what their email servers support without doing anything different than the usual. They can still attach a large file using Sharefile plugin and send the mail to the recipient. It also provides an option to upload the file on their server and then share the link to the file with the intended recipients. It facilitates file access from anywhere and any device for the recipient. Files are always secured by encryption while they are stored in their servers as well as during transit. One can be in complete control of their content and change the access permissions anytime from anywhere.
cons: It doesn't provide a feature to add another layer of protection such as 2 Way Authentication which can make the user feel more secure rather than just rely on Sharefile's security expertise. Also, it doesn't have a software client which can be installed on the machines which hampers the ability for all the contents in a folder to be in sync at all the devices.
